name: Babette's Feast (Babettes Gæstebud)
description: A French refugee becomes a housekeeper for two pious sisters in 19th-century Jutland. When she wins the lottery, she spends it all on preparing a magnificent French feast. Won the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.
awards: Academy Award Best Foreign Language Film 1988

name: Melancholia
description: Two sisters find their relationship challenged as a rogue planet threatens to collide with Earth. A visually stunning meditation on depression and the end of the world.

name: The Square
description: A provocative satirical comedy set in a prestigious Stockholm contemporary art museum — co-produced with Denmark, this Palme d'Or winner skewers art world pretension.

name: A War (Krigen)
description: A Danish commander in Afghanistan makes a split-second decision to protect his troops — with devastating consequences back home. Oscar-nominated for Best Foreign Language Film.
awards: Oscar Nomination Best Foreign Language Film 2016

name: Borgen (Series)
description: Although a TV series, Borgen became Denmark's most internationally celebrated political drama — following a female Prime Minister navigating power. Inspired a generation of political dramas worldwide.

name: The Hunt (Jagten)
description: A teacher's life is torn apart when a child's lie about him spreads through a small Danish community. Mads Mikkelsen's greatest performance — harrowing and essential.

name: Another Round (Druk)
description: Four high school teachers test a theory that humans function better with a constant low level of alcohol in their blood. Won the Academy Award for Best International Feature Film 2021.
awards: Academy Award Best International Feature Film 2021

name: Riders of Justice (Retfærdighedens ryttere)
description: A soldier returns home after his wife dies in a train accident and teams up with a group of quirky data analysts who believe it was no accident. Starring Mads Mikkelsen.
